title: 赛道分化加剧，2026年人工智能最强风口来袭
summary: 不再是"AI+"的修修补补，而是AI原生重构系统底层逻辑。文章分析了AI原生应用、物理AI、多模态大模型和世界模型四大趋势，认为2026年AI正从数字世界迈向物理世界，从参数规模竞赛转向对物理世界底层秩序的理解。

title: 2026年：AI开启"物理扎根"新时代
summary: 进入2026年，人工智能的主流应用从数字领域的生成与对话转向物理领域的感知应用。物理AI的核心是通过嵌入式智能连接数字世界与物理环境，实现从"感知"到"行动"的跨越。文章以国家电网为案例，展示了AI在工业领域的实际应用。

title: 不只具身智能和AI，2026年的热门风口有哪些？
summary: 文章分析了2026年五大核心风口：具身智能与人形机器人商业化、AI Agent产业重构、低空经济规模化爆发、合成生物与精准医疗融合、以及新型储能与虚拟电厂。特斯拉Optimus、Figure AI及国内优必选等企业预计在2026年实现千台级批量交付。

title: 2026，AI才是真革命
summary: 文章深度剖析了AI落地的商业现实。C端方面，字节豆包、DeepSeek周活已破亿，但用户对AI的新鲜感变成了"功能白嫖"的习惯。2026年的核心转型包括推理成本崩塌、"硅基员工"大规模入职等趋势。

title: 请回答2026：38位中国AI关键人物的Magic Moment和趋势判断
summary: 甲子光年采访了李开复、周鸿祎、稚晖君等38位中国AI关键人物。几乎没有人再把2026年视作"更大的模型之年"，取而代之的是对多模态模型、长期记忆突破、多智能体协作和物理世界闭环的关注。李开复认为2026年是"企业多智能体上岗元年"。

title: Vibe Check: Opus 4.7 Stopped Reading Between the Lines
summary: Opus 4.7 is the best coding model Every has tested on well-specified tasks, but it won't infer what you want the way 4.6 did. The gap between a tight brief and a loose one is wider than in any prior Opus. Prompts tuned for 4.6 will likely disappoint at first.

title: The Folder Is the Agent
summary: Kieran Klaassen realized after three months trying to make AI agent swarms work that a project directory with a CLAUDE.md, accumulated context, and specialized sub-agents is all you need to turn a general model into a domain expert. He's now running 44 of them connected by a Ruby dispatch layer.

title: Sparkle: Marie Kondo Your Mac (Rebuilt)
summary: Yash Poojary rebuilt Sparkle to purge the ~80% of files on the average Mac that are screenshots, installer packages, and duplicates. The new version runs a cleanup pass first, then proposes a custom folder structure you can reshape through chat.

title: Mini-Vibe Check: Claude Managed Agents Handle the Infrastructure Work
summary: Dan Shipper sits down with Eve Bodnia, founder of Logical Intelligence, who argues that LLMs have a ceiling and energy-based models scanning the full landscape of possible answers are what comes next. Plus a Mini-Vibe Check on Anthropic's Claude Managed Agents.

title: "Intention is What We Need": Neeru Khosla on the Future of Education and Learning with AI
summary: Interview with Neeru Khosla, co-founder of CK-12 Foundation, on how AI is transforming education. CK-12's Flexi AI tutor has been used by 50M+ students with 150M+ questions processed. Khosla argues AI's real value in education is understanding how students think, not just providing answers. AI literacy may become as foundational as reading and writing.

title: The Two Sides of OpenClaw: Peter Steinberger's TED and AIE Talks
summary: Peter Steinberger's TED talk and AIE talk about OpenClaw dropped. The TED talk told the inspiring story while the AIE talk covered the sober reality: 60x more security reports than curl, at least 20% of skill contributions malicious, and unprecedented scaling issues for the fastest growing open source project in history.

title: Claude Design Launches as Anthropic's First Design/Prototyping Surface
summary: Anthropic launched Claude Design, a research-preview tool for generating prototypes, slides, and one-pagers from natural-language instructions, powered by Claude Opus 4.7. Multiple observers called it a direct shot at Figma/Lovable/Bolt/v0. Figma's stock saw a sharp drawdown after the announcement.

title: Opus 4.7 Benchmarks: #1 in Code Arena, Near Three-Way Tie at Top Overall
summary: Opus 4.7 placed #1 in Code Arena (+37 over Opus 4.6) and #1 overall in Text Arena. ArtificialAnalysis reported a near three-way tie: Opus 4.7 57.3, Gemini 3.1 Pro 57.2, GPT-5.4 56.8. Opus 4.7 uses ~35% fewer output tokens than Opus 4.6 at higher score, with task budgets and removal of extended thinking in favor of adaptive reasoning.

title: Codex Computer Use: OpenAI's Desktop Agent Becomes Usable for Enterprise
summary: OpenAI's Codex desktop/computer-use updates drew strong practitioner reactions. It can drive Slack, browser flows, and arbitrary desktop apps, and may be the first genuinely usable computer-use platform for enterprise legacy software. Greg Brockman explicitly framed Codex as becoming a full agentic IDE.

title: Agent Harnesses > Fancy AI Scaffolds: Field Converges on Simple Patterns
summary: Multiple high-signal posts argued reliability gains now come more from harnesses than chasing larger models. The leaked Claude Code harness shows simple planning constraints plus cleaner representation outperform fancy AI scaffolds. Qwen3-8B scored 33/507 on LongCoT-Mini with dspy.RLM versus 0/507 vanilla—the scaffold did 100% of the lifting.

title: Ollama Ships Native Hermes Agent Support
summary: Ollama shipped native Hermes Agent support via 'ollama launch hermes'. Nous Research amplified the launch. Nous and Kimi also launched a $25k Hermes Agent Creative Hackathon, signaling a push from coding/productivity into creative agent workflows.

title: Cognitive Companion: Monitoring Reasoning Degradation in Agents
summary: A logistic-regression probe on layer-28 hidden states can detect reasoning degradation with AUROC 0.840 at zero inference overhead, while an LLM-monitor version cuts repetition 52–62% with ~11% overhead. Separate WebXSkill work extracts reusable skills from agent trajectories, yielding +9.8 points on WebArena.

title: Stargate on Track for 9+ GW by 2029, Comparable to NYC Peak Demand
summary: Epoch AI Research surveyed all 7 US Stargate sites and concluded the project appears on track for 9+ GW by 2029, comparable to New York City peak demand. Today's annual global datacenter capex is roughly 5–7 Manhattan Projects per year in inflation-adjusted terms.

title: Qwen3.6 Local/Quantized Workflows: A Practical Bright Spot
summary: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B is running well as a local agent stack via llama.cpp + Pi. Red Hat released an NVFP4-quantized checkpoint with GSM8K 100.69% recovery. Consumer-hardware inference keeps improving with PyTorch/TorchAO enabling FP8/NVFP4 quant offloading without major latency penalties.

title: PyCon US 2026 in Long Beach: New AI and Security Tracks
summary: PyCon US 2026 will feature two new dedicated tracks: an AI track and a Security track. The AI track includes 8 talks covering topics from AI-assisted contributions, quantization, browser-based edge inference, async patterns for AI agents, and building real-time voice agents in Python.

title: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B on Laptop Beats Claude Opus 4.7 at SVG Pelican Benchmark
summary: Simon Willison's pelican-on-bicycle SVG benchmark shows Qwen3.6-35B-A3B (20.9GB GGUF via LM Studio on MacBook Pro M5) produced a better pelican than Claude Opus 4.7. Even on a flamingo-on-unicycle backup test, Qwen won. This breaks the previous correlation between pelican quality and model usefulness.

title: SQLite 3.53.0: Major Release with ALTER TABLE Improvements
summary: SQLite 3.53.0 brings significant improvements: ALTER TABLE can now add and remove NOT NULL and CHECK constraints, new json_array_insert() function, and significant CLI mode improvements including result formatting via a new Query Results Formatter library.

title: Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS: Text-to-Speech with Prompt-Based Direction
summary: Google released Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS, a prompt-directed text-to-speech model. It uses elaborate character/scene prompts to control voice style, accent, pacing, and delivery. The prompting approach supports different regional accents like London, Newcastle, and Devon.

title: Cybersecurity Now Looks Like Proof of Work: Claude Mythos and GPT-5.4-Cyber
summary: The UK AI Safety Institute's evaluation of Claude Mythos confirms it is exceptionally effective at finding security vulnerabilities, with effectiveness scaling linearly with compute/tokens spent. OpenAI responded with GPT-5.4-Cyber, a fine-tuned model for defensive cybersecurity. Open source libraries become more valuable since security tokens can be shared across all users.

title: Google Engineering AI Adoption: Same as John Deere?
summary: Steve Yegge claimed Google's internal AI adoption mirrors the industry curve: 20% agentic power users, 20% refusers, 60% using basic chat tools like Cursor. Addy Osmani countered that 40K+ SWEs use agentic coding weekly at Google. Demis Hassabis called it 'pure clickbait'.

title: Datasette 1.0a27-a28: CSRF Protection Overhaul Using Modern Browser Headers
summary: Datasette replaced Django-style CSRF tokens with Sec-Fetch-Site header protection inspired by Go 1.25 and Filippo Valsorda's research. This simplifies form and API integration. A follow-up a28 release fixed accidental breakages, implemented using Claude Code and Claude Opus 4.7.

title: Scaling How We Build and Test Our Most Advanced AI
summary: Meta detailed its updated Advanced AI Scaling Framework and Safety & Preparedness Report for Muse Spark. The company is mapping potential risks and evaluating models before and after safeguards are applied, testing for cybersecurity, chemical/biological threats, and safety policy compliance.

title: SAM 3.1: Faster Real-Time Video Detection and Tracking
summary: Meta released SAM 3.1 with multiplexing and global reasoning capabilities for faster and more accessible real-time video detection and tracking. The model builds on the Segment Anything foundation, extending from images to video with improved performance.

title: TRIBE v2: A Predictive Foundation Model for Brain Processing
summary: Meta introduced TRIBE v2, a predictive foundation model trained to understand how the human brain processes complex stimuli. This research represents Meta's push into neuroscience-inspired AI, bridging cognitive science and machine learning.

title: Four MTIA Chips in Two Years: Scaling AI Experiences for Billions
summary: Meta unveiled its fourth custom MTIA AI chip in just two years, scaling AI inference infrastructure for billions of users. The custom silicon approach allows Meta to optimize AI workloads specific to its recommendation, content understanding, and generative AI needs.

title: Canopy Height Maps v2: Mapping the World's Forests
summary: In partnership with the World Resources Institute, Meta released Canopy Height Maps v2, an open source model and world-scale maps for forest monitoring. The model enables more precise global forest mapping for environmental conservation.

title: How DINO and SAM Help Modernize Medical Triage
summary: Teams at the University of Pennsylvania are leveraging Meta's DINOv2 and SAM models to bring cutting-edge automation to emergency response triage. The AI-powered system aims to improve the speed and accuracy of medical assessments in emergency settings.

title: How Alta Daily Uses Meta's Segment Anything to Reimagine the Digital Closet
summary: Alta Daily is using Meta's Segment Anything model to reimagine the digital closet experience in fashion tech. The application demonstrates practical commercial uses of Meta's open-source computer vision models.

title: Reducing Government Costs with DINO in the UK
summary: The UK government is using Meta's DINOv2 model to enhance reforestation efforts and increase access to greenspaces while reducing costs. This case study shows how open-source AI models can improve public sector efficiency.

title: Microsoft launches three foundation AI models
summary: Microsoft announced three new foundation AI models optimized for different scenarios, providing developers with more powerful generative tools. The company also plans to complete its frontier large model development in-house by 2027, signaling increased independence from OpenAI.

title: Microsoft MAI models: Transcribe, Voice, and Image
summary: Microsoft launched three world-class AI models built entirely in-house, covering transcription, voice, and image capabilities. This marks a significant step toward Microsoft's AI independence from OpenAI, developing proprietary models for key modalities.

title: Microsoft Copilot Cowork
summary: Microsoft launched Copilot Cowork, an AI assistant that doesn't just help users work but actively does the work for them. The product represents Microsoft's deepest integration of AI agents into productivity workflows, with end-to-end task completion capabilities.

title: Microsoft invests $5.5 billion in Singapore AI
summary: Microsoft is investing $5.5 billion in Singapore for regional AI infrastructure, setting a new record for the company in Asia-Pacific. Students will receive free Copilot subscriptions to assist with digital transformation in education.

title: Microsoft open sources MarkItDown universal file converter
summary: Microsoft open-sourced a universal file conversion library supporting Office to Markdown and YouTube content extraction. The tool has become essential for AI training data preparation, with GitHub stars growing rapidly.

summary: • Congress should ensure that it does not preempt states from enforcing their own generally applicable laws protecting children, such as prohibitions on child sexual abuse material, even where such material is generated by AI. • Congress should consider establishing a federal framework protecting individuals from the unauthorized distribution or commercial use of AI-generated digital replicas of their voice, likeness, or other identifiable attributes, while providing clear exceptions for parody, satire, news reporting, and other expressive works protected by the First Amendment. • Congress should preempt state AI laws that impose undue burdens to ensure a minimally burdensome national standard consistent with these recommendations, not fifty discordant ones. • This national standard should respect key principles of federalism and not preempt: o The traditional police powers retained by the states to enforce laws of general applicability against AI developers and users, including particular laws to protect children, prevent fraud, and protect consumers.

title: White House Releases a National Policy Framework for Artificial ...
summary: [Skip to content](https://www.hklaw.com/en/insights/publications/2026/03/white-house-releases-a-national-policy-framework-for-artificial#content). *   The White House released a National Policy Framework for Artificial Intelligence (AI) (Framework), outlining nonbinding legislative recommendations to inform congressional consideration of a unified federal approach to AI regulation. *   This Holland & Knight alert reviews the Framework's core policy positions and situates them alongside related congressional proposals, including U.S. Sen. Marsha Blackburn's updated TRUMP AMERICA AI Act. The White House released a [National Policy Framework for Artificial Intelligence](https://www.whitehouse.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/03.20.26-National-Policy-Framework-for-Artificial-Intelligence-Legislative-Recommendations.pdf) (Framework) on March 20, 2026, outlining legislative recommendations intended to guide U.S. Congress as it considers federal artificial intelligence (AI) legislation. The Trump Administration issued "[Removing Barriers to American Leadership in Artificial Intelligence](https://www.whitehouse.gov/presidential-actions/2025/12/eliminating-state-law-obstruction-of-national-artificial-intelligence-policy/)" (EO 14365) on December 11, 2025, which initiated a coordinated federal review of state-level AI laws and directed agencies to develop policy recommendations, standards and legislative proposals to inform a national approach. Our [HK Health AI Navigator](https://www.hklaw.com/en/general-pages/holland-knight-health-artificial-intelligence-navigator) tracks and interprets AI-related laws and policies impacting the healthcare industry.

title: White House, Blackburn Introduce Visions of Comprehensive Federal AI Policy | Global Policy Watch
summary: The framework recommends that Congress preempt state AI laws that “impose undue burdens,” including state AI laws that “govern areas better suited” to federal regulation or that are “contrary to the United States’ national strategy to achieve global AI dominance.”  Specifically, the framework calls for the preemption of state laws that (1) “regulate AI development,” which is “an inherently interstate phenomenon”; (2) “unduly burden Americans’ use of AI” for otherwise lawful activities; or (3) “penalize AI developers” for unlawful third-party conduct involving their AI models. Consistent with the President’s AI Preemption Executive Order, which noted that its “legislative recommendation” should not preempt state AI laws related to child safety, AI infrastructure, or state procurement and use (among “other topics as shall be determined”), the framework calls for Congress to “respect key principles of federalism” by not preempting (1) state “traditional police powers” to enforce “laws of general applicability” against AI developers and users, including safety for minors, consumer protection, and CSAM laws; (2) state zoning laws for AI infrastructure; or (3) requirements “governing a state’s own use of AI.”.

title: 2026 is Breakthrough Year for Reliable AI World Models and ...
summary: \Demis Hassabis (DeepMind CEO) and other AI leaders sees the next big AI gains—and the path to AGI—will come from targeted algorithmic breakthroughs in areas like continual learning, memory architectures, world models, reasoning/planning, and hybrid systems. – Broader consensus (Altman, Amodei, Karpathy, Dean) echoes the shift to inference-time scaling, agentic loops, memory augmentation, and closing the reality gap via simulation. – Inference scaling (o1-style reasoning chains, test-time training). – 2026 Breakthrough year for **reliable world models + continual learning prototypes**. – 2028+ Potential for more autonomous self-improvement loops if memory/world-model gaps close. Hassabis and others see this as the point where 1–2 breakthroughs compound into AGI-level consistency across reasoning, creativity, and real-world interaction. Hassabis and the frontier are clear—the era of just scale LLMs is transitioning to memory-augmented, world-model-driven, continually learning agentic systems. Scaling buys time and capability, but the algorithmic innovations (Nested Learning, Titans/Hope, Genie 3, inference-time reasoning) are the real accelerators for 2026–2028.

summary: The artificial intelligence coding revolution comes with a catch: it's expensive. Claude Code , Anthropic's terminal-based AI agent that can write, debug, and deploy code autonomously, has captured the imagination of software developers worldwide. But its pricing — ranging from $20 to $200 per month depending on usage — has sparked a growing rebellion among the very programmers it aims to serve. Now, a free alternative is gaining traction.

title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
summary: Alfred Wahlforss was running out of options. His startup, Listen Labs , needed to hire over 100 engineers, but competing against Mark Zuckerberg's $100 million offers seemed impossible. So he spent $5,000 — a fifth of his marketing budget — on a billboard in San Francisco displaying what looked like gibberish: five strings of random numbers. The numbers were actually AI tokens.
